methods.method_name: Assessment of Female Flower and Fruit Production
methods.description: This method assesses the reproductive output of female Lodoicea maldivica trees by examining their inflorescences, which provide a record of reproductive history over a 7-year period. The method aims to determine the number of flowers produced, the number of ovules fertilized, and the number of fruits that developed normally.

steps.description: Examine the oldest inflorescence to determine the number of flowers produced seven years previously.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine oldest inflorescence to count flowers produced seven years ago

steps.description: Determine how many ovules were fertilized by examining the inflorescence.
steps.searchable_summary: Count fertilized ovules from inflorescence examination

steps.description: Identify how many of the fertilized ovules developed normally into mature fruits.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify normally developed mature fruits from fertilized ovules

steps.description: Look for bowl-shaped scars surrounded by lignified perianth parts to confirm that a mature fruit has been shed.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect bowl-shaped scars to confirm shed mature fruits

steps.description: Occasionally, identify similar scars on younger inflorescences to indicate that an immature fruit has been shed.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify scars on younger inflorescences for shed immature fruits
